files navigation # Shopify app tutorial with admin extensions [Permalink: Shopify app tutorial with admin extensions](https://github.com/Shopify/example-admin--action-and-block--react#shopify-app-tutorial-with-admin-extensions) This app is a guide for adding extensions to a Shopify app. Rather than cloning this repo, you can use your preferred package manager and the Shopify CLI with [these steps](https://shopify.dev/docs/apps/getting-started/create) to create your own app and these [tutorials](https://shopify.dev/docs/apps/admin/admin-actions-and-blocks) to get started with admin action and block extensions. This repo tracks the four-part tutorial series that covers: - [Building an admin action extension](https://shopify.dev/docs/apps/admin/admin-actions-and-blocks/build-an-admin-action) - [Building an admin block extension](https://shopify.dev/docs/apps/admin/admin-actions-and-blocks/build-an-admin-block) - [Connecting the action and block extensions](https://shopify.dev/docs/apps/admin/admin-actions-and-blocks/connect-action-and-block) - [Connecting an extension to your app's backend](https://shopify.dev/docs/apps/admin/admin-actions-and-blocks/connect-extension-and-backend) ## Aligning this app to the tutorial [Permalink: Aligning this app to the tutorial](https://github.com/Shopify/example-admin--action-and-block--react#aligning-this-app-to-the-tutorial) Running this app with no changes, will start you at the finishing point of [Building an admin block extension](https://shopify.dev/docs/apps/admin/admin-actions-and-blocks/build-an-admin-block). This is the point where we have created an action and a block extension, but they are not yet connected to each other or fetching data from a backend. If you would like to run the app and extensions in another state, you will need to make some small adjustments in the configuration. ### Connected extensions [Permalink: Connected extensions](https://github.com/Shopify/example-admin--action-and-block--react#connected-extensions) To run the extensions in the state that they are in at the finishing point of [Connecting the action and block extensions](https://shopify.dev/docs/apps/admin/admin-actions-and-blocks/connect-action-and-block), change the module path value in `extensions/issue-tracker-action/shopify.extension.toml` to `./src/ActionExtension-connect.jsx`. ``` -module = "./src/ActionExtension.jsx" +module = "./src/ActionExtension-connect.jsx" ``` Additionally, change the module path value in `extensions/issue-tracker-block/shopify.extension.toml` to `./src/BlockExtension-connect.jsx`. ``` -module = "./src/BlockExtension.jsx" +module = "./src/BlockExtension-connect.jsx" ``` ### Connected to the app's backend [Permalink: Connected to the app's backend](https://github.com/Shopify/example-admin--action-and-block--react#connected-to-the-apps-backend) To run the extensions in the state that they are in at the finishing point of [Connecting an extension to your app's backend](https://shopify.dev/docs/apps/admin/admin-actions-and-blocks/connect-extension-and-backend), change the module path value in `extensions/issue-tracker-action/shopify.extension.toml` to `./src/ActionExtension-backend.jsx`. ``` -module = "./src/ActionExtension.jsx" +module = "./src/ActionExtension-backend.jsx" ``` ## A note on the comments [Permalink: A note on the comments](https://github.com/Shopify/example-admin--action-and-block--react#a-note-on-the-comments) You will find magic comments, (eg. `# [START action-extension.configuration]`) throughout the files in this app. These are for highlighting code in shopify.dev and can be ignored. ## Quick start [Permalink: Quick start](https://github.com/Shopify/example-admin--action-and-block--react#quick-start) ### Prerequisites [Permalink: Prerequisites](https://github.com/Shopify/example-admin--action-and-block--react#prerequisites) 1. You must [download and install Node.js](https://nodejs.org/en/download/) if you don't already have it. 2. You must [create a Shopify partner account](https://partners.shopify.com/signup) if you don’t have one. 3. You must create a store for testing if you don't have one, either a [development store](https://help.shopify.com/en/partners/dashboard/development-stores#create-a-development-store) or a [Shopify Plus sandbox store](https://help.shopify.com/en/partners/dashboard/managing-stores/plus-sandbox-store). ### Setup [Permalink: Setup](https://github.com/Shopify/example-admin--action-and-block--react#setup) If you used the CLI to create the template, you can skip this section. Using yarn: ``` yarn install ``` Using npm: ``` npm install ``` Using pnpm: ``` pnpm install ``` ### Local Development [Permalink: Local Development](https://github.com/Shopify/example-admin--action-and-block--react#local-development) Using yarn: ``` yarn dev ``` Using npm: ``` npm run dev ``` Using pnpm: ``` pnpm run dev ``` Press P to open the URL to your app. Once you click install, you can start development. Local development is powered by [the Shopify CLI](https://shopify.dev/docs/apps/tools/cli). It logs into your partners account, connects to an app, provides environment variables, updates remote config, creates a tunnel and provides commands to generate extensions. ## More about the app [Permalink: More about the app](https://github.com/Shopify/example-admin--action-and-block--react#more-about-the-app) For more information about the base Remix app without extensions, check out this [repo](https://github.com/Shopify/shopify-app-template-remix). ## Resources [Permalink: Resources](https://github.com/Shopify/example-admin--action-and-block--react#resources) - [Remix Docs](https://remix.run/docs/en/v1) - [Shopify App Remix](https://shopify.dev/docs/api/shopify-app-remix) - [Introduction to Shopify apps](https://shopify.dev/docs/apps/getting-started) - [App authentication](https://shopify.dev/docs/apps/auth) - [Shopify CLI](https://shopify.dev/docs/apps/tools/cli) - [App extensions](https://shopify.dev/docs/apps/app-extensions/list) - [Shopify Functions](https://shopify.dev/docs/api/functions) - [Getting started with internationalizing your app](https://shopify.dev/docs/apps/best-practices/internationalization/getting-started) ## About Contains example code for tutorials at [https://shopify.dev/docs/apps/admin/admin-actions-and-blocks](https://shopify.dev/docs/apps/admin/admin-actions-and-blocks) ### Resources [Readme](https://github.com/Shopify/example-admin--action-and-block--react#readme-ov-file) ### License [MIT license](https://github.com/Shopify/example-admin--action-and-block--react#MIT-1-ov-file) ### Code of conduct [Code of conduct](https://github.com/Shopify/example-admin--action-and-block--react#coc-ov-file) ### Security policy [Security policy](https://github.com/Shopify/example-admin--action-and-block--react#security-ov-file) ### Uh oh! # [Shopify Admin API Tutorial | Polaris and React App UI/UX](https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=ckSilldzfks)

**Visibility**: Public
**Uploaded by**: [Stephnn0](http://www.youtube.com/@stephanodev)
**Uploaded at**: 2023-01-29T09:23:57-08:00
**Published at**: 2023-01-29T09:23:57-08:00
**Length**: 35:14
**Views**: 12738
**Likes**: 199
**Category**: People & Blogs

## Description

```
In this tutorial we are going to build a custom app for Shopify website integration. 

This is the second part from our series. 
Please check the first part if you haven't watch it.

Part 1 : https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=hJCjYdxySIw&t=3618s

We are going to walk through the documentation as well as working some of the new features of the new Shopify CLI-3.

This video will show you how to apply polaris for UI/UX design to our app.

You will also learn to apply navigation with react router.

#webdevelopment 
#shopify 
#react 
#coding 
#programming 
#nodejs 
#javascript 
#liquid 
#api 

Hope you gain value from this tutorial! I will try to start a series for shopify developers and dive deep into this great framework! 

Thank you for watching! 

github: https://github.com/stefanoHTB/bulk_order_shopify
```

## Transcript

hello guys welcome to another Shopify tutorial and welcome to my channel again today uh we are going to be continuing with our series with Shopify and to be more specific we're going to start building the UI of our app uh if you haven't checked out my last tutorial I suggest you go and watch it before this one because that one is more of an introductory tutorial for Shopify so anyways uh here's the Shopify documentation we're going to just like last time we're going to walk through it uh work together with the documentation because it's the best thing to do in my opinion we're going to be Building a Shopify Website with a React UI Framework: A Comprehensive Guide [![Sampath](https://miro.medium.com/v2/da:true/resize:fill:32:32/0*ks71yt2j3n7eCOyn)](https://medium.com/@sampath.theekoder?source=post_page---byline--ab7afc201d68---------------------------------------) [Sampath](https://medium.com/@sampath.theekoder?source=post_page---byline--ab7afc201d68---------------------------------------) Follow 17 min read · Jan 1, 2024 [Listen](https://medium.com/m/signin?actionUrl=https%3A%2F%2Fmedium.com%2Fplans%3Fdimension%3Dpost_audio_button%26postId%3Dab7afc201d68&operation=register&redirect=https%3A%2F%2Fmedium.com%2F%40sampath.theekoder%2Fbuilding-a-shopify-website-with-a-react-ui-framework-a-comprehensive-guide-ab7afc201d68&source=---header_actions--ab7afc201d68---------------------post_audio_button------------------) Share Press enter or click to view image in full size ![Building a Shopify Website with a React UI Framework: A Comprehensive Guide](https://miro.medium.com/v2/resize:fit:700/1*rvKB14NLO1GCaYspRDx5Jg.png) In today’s digital age, having a strong online presence is essential for any business. One of the most popular e-commerce platforms for creating a web page and selling products online is Shopify. To make your Shopify store stand out and provide a seamless user experience, integrating a robust UI framework like React JS is a fantastic choice. In this article, we’ll explore how to build a Shopify website with a React JS UI framework and dive into various aspects of front-end and back-end development to create a customized web design. ## 1\. Setting the Foundation ### Choosing Shopify as Your E-commerce Platform When it comes to establishing an online presence for your business, choosing the right e-commerce platform is a pivotal decision. Shopify emerges as an ideal choice for numerous reasons, particularly for businesses aiming to build a Shopify website with a React JS UI framework. ### Shopify’s User-Friendly Interface One of Shopify’s standout features is its user-friendly interface. This platform is designed with both beginners and experienced users in mind, making it accessible to a wide range of businesses. Whether you’re a seasoned developer or someone with minimal technical expertise, you can efficiently manage your online store on Shopify. ### Extensive App Ecosystem Shopify boasts an extensive ecosystem of apps and integrations that cater to various business needs. This ecosystem provides you with the flexibility to customize your online store, enhance its functionality, and integrate it seamlessly with your React JS UI framework. Whether you require analytics tools, payment gateways, or marketing solutions, Shopify’s App Store has you covered. ### Mobile Responsiveness In today’s mobile-centric world, having a website that performs well on smartphones and tablets is crucial. Shopify ensures that your online store is mobile-responsive out of the box, which is vital for providing an exceptional user experience and improving search engine rankings. Combining this mobile responsiveness with the capabilities of a React UI framework makes for a winning combination in creating a web page that caters to the modern user. ### SEO-Friendly Features Search engine optimization (SEO) is a critical aspect of online visibility. Shopify includes a range of built-in SEO features, such as customizable meta tags, clean URL structures, and XML sitemaps, which can help your website rank higher in search engine results. When integrated with a React JS UI framework, you can take your SEO efforts to the next level by optimizing the front-end for both users and search engines. ### Security and Hosting Security is a top priority when it comes to e-commerce websites. Shopify provides secure and reliable hosting, ensuring that your online store remains protected from potential threats. This peace of mind extends to your customers as well, as they can trust that their sensitive information is handled securely during transactions. When building your custom web design with React, you can focus on enhancing the user experience, knowing that the underlying infrastructure is robust and secure. ### Scalability As your business grows, so do your website’s requirements. Shopify offers scalability options that allow you to seamlessly expand your online store to accommodate increasing traffic and sales. When combined with a React JS UI framework, you have the flexibility to adapt your website to evolving business needs while maintaining a consistent and appealing user interface. In conclusion, Shopify’s user-friendly nature, extensive app ecosystem, mobile responsiveness, SEO-friendly features, security, hosting, and scalability make it an ideal choice for businesses of all sizes. Press enter or click to view image in full size ![](https://miro.medium.com/v2/resize:fit:700/1*Eqz6q8SmYxiR-wKcfFvdrw.png) ## Understanding the Basics of React JS When it comes to building a Shopify website with a React UI framework, a solid understanding of React JS is fundamental. React JS, often simply referred to as React, is a JavaScript library developed by Facebook. It has gained immense popularity in the web development community for its ability to create dynamic and interactive user interfaces efficiently. In this section, we’ll delve into the basics of React JS and highlight its benefits for building engaging web pages for your e-commerce business. ## What is React JS? React JS is an open-source JavaScript library used for building user interfaces, especially for single-page applications (SPAs) and web applications with complex user interactions. It was developed to address the challenges of updating and rendering parts of a web page efficiently, and it achieves this by using a component-based architecture. ### Component-Based Architecture React is built around the concept of components. A component is a self-contained, reusable piece of code that encapsulates a specific part of a user interface. Components can be as simple as a button or as complex as an entire page. This modular approach makes it easier to manage and maintain code, especially in large-scale projects. ## Benefits of Using React JS for Your Shopify Website Now that we have a basic understanding of React JS, let’s explore why it’s a fantastic choice for building the user interface of your Shopify website: ### A. Reusable Components In the context of creating a web page, React’s component-based architecture allows you to create reusable UI elements. For an e-commerce site like Shopify, this means you can build common elements like product listings, shopping carts, and navigation menus as separate components. This reusability not only speeds up development but also ensures consistency throughout your site. ### B. Virtual DOM React uses a Virtual DOM (Document Object Model) to efficiently update the user interface. When data changes, React creates a virtual representation of the DOM and compares it to the previous version. Only the differences are then applied to the actual DOM. This process significantly reduces the need for expensive DOM manipulation, resulting in faster and more responsive web pages. ### C. Declarative Syntax React utilizes a declarative syntax, making it easier to understand and maintain your code. Instead of imperatively specifying how the UI should change when data changes, you declare what the UI should look like for a given state, and React takes care of the rest. This makes your code more predictable and less error-prone. Strong Community Support React has a vast and active community of developers, which means there are plenty of resources, libraries, and tools available to help you build your Shopify website. You can find pre-built components, UI frameworks, and even state management solutions like Redux that seamlessly integrate with React. ### E. SEO-Friendly SPAs Although single-page applications (SPAs) are known for their dynamic, app-like behavior, they can sometimes pose challenges for search engine optimization (SEO). React, with the help of server-side rendering (SSR) or static site generation (SSG) techniques, can ensure that your Shopify website remains SEO-friendly while still providing a fast and engaging user experience. Back-End Development ## Express.js API Integration In this section, we’ll delve into the intricacies of integrating Express.js into your Shopify store. Express.js is a versatile and powerful Node.js framework that allows us to create RESTful APIs for efficient data management. Follow these steps to set up your back end: ### Installing Express.js To begin, you need to have Node.js installed on your system. If you haven’t already, you can download and install [Node.js](https://nodejs.org/en/download) from the official website. Once Node.js is installed, you can easily set up Express.js using npm (Node Package Manager). Open your terminal and run the following command: ``` npm install express ``` This command installs Express.js as a dependency in your project. ### Creating the Express App Now, let’s create an Express.js application. In your project directory, create a new file named `app.js` (or any other preferred name). This file will serve as the entry point for your Express application. Here's a simple example of setting up an Express app: ``` // app.js const express = require('express'); const app = express(); const port = process.env.PORT || 3000; // Define routes and middleware here app.listen(port, () => { console.log(`Server is running on port ${port}`); }); ``` In this code snippet, we import the `express` module, create an instance of the Express app, and specify a port (defaulting to 3000). You can customize the port as needed. ### Creating RESTful APIs Now, let’s create RESTful APIs for data management. For instance, if you want to manage products in your Shopify store, you can define routes for creating, reading, updating, and deleting products. Here’s an example of setting up a route for retrieving a list of products: ``` // app.js // Define a sample API route for retrieving products app.get('/api/products', (req, res) => { // Replace this with actual data retrieval logic const products = [\ { id: 1, name: 'Product 1' },\ { id: 2, name: 'Product 2' },\ // Add more products here\ ]; res.json(products); }); // ... (rest of your routes) ``` In this example, we’ve created a simple `/api/products` route that returns a list of products. You can replace the sample data with actual data retrieval and manipulation logic. ## Connecting Front-End to Back-End Now that your Express.js API is set up, it’s time to establish communication between your front-end React application and the Express.js back end. This connection allows your React app to send requests to the API and receive data. ### Making API Requests from React In your React application, you can use libraries like Axios or the built-in `fetch` API to make HTTP requests to your Express.js back end. You can use the `cors` middleware package to handle CORS. Bringing It All Together ## Testing and Debugging Testing and debugging are crucial phases in the development process to ensure that your Shopify website with the React UI framework is functioning correctly and performing optimally. Let’s explore some best practices and tools to help you achieve this. ### Unit Testing with Jest and Enzyme Unit testing is the practice of testing individual components of your React application to ensure that they work as expected. Jest and Enzyme are popular testing libraries for React applications. Here’s how you can set up Jest and Enzyme for your project: ``` npm install --save jest @testing-library/react @testing-library/jest-dom enzyme enzyme-adapter-react-16 ``` Next, configure Jest and Enzyme in your project by creating a `setupTests.js` file: ``` import { configure } from 'enzyme'; import Adapter from 'enzyme-adapter-react-16'; configure({ adapter: new Adapter() }); ``` Now, you can write unit tests for your React components. For example: ``` import React from 'react'; import { render } from '@testing-library/react'; import MyComponent from './MyComponent'; test('renders MyComponent without errors', () => { const { getByText } = render(); const element = getByText('Hello, World!'); expect(element).toBeInTheDocument(); }); ``` ### Integration Testing In addition to unit testing, it’s essential to perform integration testing to ensure that different parts of your Shopify website work together seamlessly. Tools like Cypress or React Testing Library can be valuable for integration testing. Here’s how to set up Cypress: ``` npm install --save cypress ``` Create a test script in your `package.json`: ``` "scripts": { "test:e2e": "cypress open" } ``` Then, run `npm run test:e2e` to open the Cypress test runner. ### Performance Testing To assess the performance of your Shopify website, consider using tools like Google Lighthouse or WebPageTest. These tools can analyze your website’s speed, accessibility, SEO, and more. Make any necessary changes and click “Save.” Shopify handles the hosting and deployment for you, making it a user-friendly option. ### Other Hosting Providers If you prefer more control and flexibility over your hosting environment, consider using a cloud-based hosting provider like AWS (Amazon Web Services), Google Cloud, or Microsoft Azure. Approach explored: App Sections and App Snippets (theme app extensions that inject Liquid sections/snippets) vs. Shopify ScriptTag API (adds external JavaScript to storefront pages). Latest update: the poster concluded it’s not possible to use App Sections/App Snippets for this React component on the product page and suggested using Shopify’s ScriptTag API as the best workaround. Follow-ups: other participants asked whether it was successfully implemented and requested details on how to do it with ScriptTag, but no steps or code were shared. Outcome: no concrete implementation guidance provided; the thread remains open with unresolved questions. Notes: no images, videos, or code snippets were included. web.description: Polaris Web Components are now the default way to build Shopify apps. Built on web standards, they work with React, Vue, vanilla JavaScript, or no framework at ...
web.markdown:
[**Introducing Polaris Web Components** Now Released](https://shopify.dev/docs/api/app-home/polaris-web-components)

[Skip to content](https://polaris-react.shopify.com/components#main) [![Shopify logo](https://polaris-react.shopify.com/images/shopify-logo.svg)Polaris React](https://polaris-react.shopify.com/) 🌙Search /

# Components

Components are the reusable building blocks for creating Shopify admin experiences.

## Actions

- [![Account connection](https://polaris-react.shopify.com/_next/image?url=%2Fimages%2Fcomponents%2Factions%2Faccount-connection.png&w=3840&q=75)\\
\\
**Account connection** \\
Used for connecting or disconnecting a store to various accounts, like Facebook for the sales channel.](https://polaris-react.shopify.com/components/actions/account-connection)
- [![Button](https://polaris-react.shopify.com/_next/image?url=%2Fimages%2Fcomponents%2Factions%2Fbutton.png&w=3840&q=75)\\
\\
**Button** \\
Used primarily for actions like 'Add', 'Close', 'Cancel', or 'Save'. Plain buttons are used for less important actions.](https://polaris-react.shopify.com/components/actions/button)
- [![Button group](https://polaris-react.shopify.com/_next/image?url=%2Fimages%2Fcomponents%2Factions%2Fbutton-group.png&w=3840&q=75)\\
\\
**Button group** \\
Displays multiple related actions stacked or in a horizontal row for arrangement and spacing.](https://polaris-react.shopify.com/components/actions/button-group)

## Layout and structure

- [![Bleed](https://polaris-react.shopify.com/_next/image?url=%2Fimages%2Fcomponents%2Flayout-and-structure%2Fbleed.png&w=3840&q=75)\\
\\
**Bleed** \\
Applies negative margin to a layout, extending it to the edge of the screen on small screens.](https://polaris-react.shopify.com/components/layout-and-structure/bleed)
- [![Block stack](https://polaris-react.shopify.com/_next/image?url=%2Fimages%2Fcomponents%2Flayout-and-structure%2Fblock-stack.png&w=3840&q=75)\\
\\
**Block stack** \\
Use to display children vertically and horizontally with full width by default. Based on CSS Flexbox.](https://polaris-react.shopify.com/components/layout-and-structure/block-stack)
- [![Box](https://polaris-react.shopify.com/_next/image?url=%2Fimages%2Fcomponents%2Flayout-and-structure%2Fbox.png&w=3840&q=75)\\
\\
**Box** \\
Box is the most primitive layout component. It’s a way to access Polaris design tokens.](https://polaris-react.shopify.com/components/layout-and-structure/box)
- [![Callout card](https://polaris-react.shopify.com/_next/image?url=%2Fimages%2Fcomponents%2Flayout-and-structure%2Fcallout-card.png&w=3840&q=75)\\
\\
**Callout card** \\
Callout cards are used to encourage merchants to take an action related to a new feature or opportunity. They are most commonly displayed in the sales channels section of Shopify.](https://polaris-react.shopify.com/components/layout-and-structure/callout-card)
- [![Card](https://polaris-react.shopify.com/_next/image?url=%2Fimages%2Fcomponents%2Flayout-and-structure%2Fcard.png&w=3840&q=75)\\
\\
**Card** \\
Used to group similar concepts and tasks together for easier scanning and reading.](https://polaris-react.shopify.com/components/layout-and-structure/card)
- [![Divider](https://polaris-react.shopify.com/_next/image?url=%2Fimages%2Fcomponents%2Flayout-and-structure%2Fdivider.png&w=3840&q=75)\\
\\
**Divider** \\
Use to separate or group content.](https://polaris-react.shopify.com/components/layout-and-structure/divider)
- [![Empty state](https://polaris-react.shopify.com/_next/image?url=%2Fimages%2Fcomponents%2Flayout-and-structure%2Fempty-state.png&w=3840&q=75)\\
\\
**Empty state** \\
Empty states are used when a list, table, or chart has no items or data to show. This is an opportunity to provide explanation or guidance to help merchants progress. The empty state component is intended for use when a full page in the admin is empty, and not for individual elements or areas in the interface.](https://polaris-react.shopify.com/components/layout-and-structure/empty-state)
- [![Form layout](https://polaris-react.shopify.com/_next/image?url=%2Fimages%2Fcomponents%2Flayout-and-structure%2Fform-layout.png&w=3840&q=75)\\
\\
**Form layout** \\
Manages the layout of all forms and fields within it. Used for the layout of new forms and managing the layout of all forms.](https://polaris-react.shopify.com/components/layout-and-structure/form-layout)
- [![Grid](https://polaris-react.shopify.com/_next/image?url=%2Fimages%2Fcomponents%2Flayout-and-structure%2Fgrid.png&w=3840&q=75)\\
\\
**Grid** \\
Create complex layouts based on CSS Grid.](https://polaris-react.shopify.com/components/layout-and-structure/grid)
- [![Inline grid](https://polaris-react.shopify.com/_next/image?url=%2Fimages%2Fcomponents%2Flayout-and-structure%2Finline-grid.png&w=3840&q=75)\\
\\
**Inline grid** \\
Use to lay out children horizontally with equal gap between columns. Based on CSS Grid.](https://polaris-react.shopify.com/components/layout-and-structure/inline-grid)
- [![Inline stack](https://polaris-react.shopify.com/_next/image?url=%2Fimages%2Fcomponents%2Flayout-and-structure%2Finline-stack.png&w=3840&q=75)\\
\\
**Inline stack** \\
Use to display children horizontally in a row. Based on CSS Flexbox.](https://polaris-react.shopify.com/components/layout-and-structure/inline-stack)
- [![Layout](https://polaris-react.shopify.com/_next/image?url=%2Fimages%2Fcomponents%2Flayout-and-structure%2Flayout.png&w=3840&q=75)\\
\\
**Layout** \\
A structural component used to group other components. Creates consistent spacing and helps layout stack and scale responsively.](https://polaris-react.shopify.com/components/layout-and-structure/layout)
- [![Media card](https://polaris-react.shopify.com/_next/image?url=%2Fimages%2Fcomponents%2Flayout-and-structure%2Fmedia-card.png&w=3840&q=75)\\
\\
**Media card** \\
Provides a container for introductory or highlight information. Often used in a grid to present related content.](https://polaris-react.shopify.com/components/layout-and-structure/media-card)
- [![Page](https://polaris-react.shopify.com/_next/image?url=%2Fimages%2Fcomponents%2Flayout-and-structure%2Fpage.png&w=3840&q=75)\\
\\
**Page** \\
Used to build the layout of a page in the Shopify admin. A flexible container for composing pages consistently.](https://polaris-react.shopify.com/components/layout-and-structure/page)

## Selection and input

- [![Autocomplete](https://polaris-react.shopify.com/_next/image?url=%2Fimages%2Fcomponents%2Fselection-and-input%2Fautocomplete.png&w=3840&q=75)\\
\\
**Autocomplete** \\
The autocomplete component is an input field that provides selectable suggestions as a merchant types into it. It allows merchants to quickly search through and select from large collections of options. It's a convenience wrapper around the Combobox and Listbox components with minor UI differences.](https://polaris-react.shopify.com/components/selection-and-input/autocomplete)
- [![Checkbox](https://polaris-react.shopify.com/_next/image?url=%2Fimages%2Fcomponents%2Fselection-and-input%2Fcheckbox.png&w=3840&q=75)\\
\\
**Checkbox** \\
Checkboxes are most commonly used to give merchants a way to make a range of selections (zero, one, or multiple). They may also be used as a way to have merchants indicate they agree to specific terms and services.](https://polaris-react.shopify.com/components/selection-and-input/checkbox)
- [![Choice list](https://polaris-react.shopify.com/_next/image?url=%2Fimages%2Fcomponents%2Fselection-and-input%2Fchoice-list.png&w=3840&q=75)\\
\\
**Choice list** \\
A choice list lets you create a list of grouped radio buttons or checkboxes. Use this component if you need to group together a related list of interactive choices.](https://polaris-react.shopify.com/components/selection-and-input/choice-list)
- [![Color picker](https://polaris-react.shopify.com/_next/image?url=%2Fimages%2Fcomponents%2Fselection-and-input%2Fcolor-picker.png&w=3840&q=75)\\
\\
**Color picker** \\
Allows merchants to choose a color visually, or by entering a hex value.](https://polaris-react.shopify.com/components/selection-and-input/color-picker)
- [![Combobox](https://polaris-react.shopify.com/_next/image?url=%2Fimages%2Fcomponents%2Fselection-and-input%2Fcombobox.png&w=3840&q=75)\\
\\
**Combobox** \\
Combobox is an accessible autocomplete input that enables merchants to filter a list of options and select one or more values.](https://polaris-react.shopify.com/components/selection-and-input/combobox)
- [![Date picker](https://polaris-react.shopify.com/_next/image?url=%2Fimages%2Fcomponents%2Fselection-and-input%2Fdate-picker.png&w=3840&q=75)\\
\\
**Date picker** \\
Date pickers let merchants choose dates from a visual calendar that’s consistently applied wherever dates need to be selected across Shopify.](https://polaris-react.shopify.com/components/selection-and-input/date-picker)
- [![Drop zone](https://polaris-react.shopify.com/_next/image?url=%2Fimages%2Fcomponents%2Fselection-and-input%2Fdrop-zone.png&w=3840&q=75)\\
\\
**Drop zone** \\
The drop zone component lets users upload files by dragging and dropping the files into an area on a page, or activating a button.](https://polaris-react.shopify.com/components/selection-and-input/drop-zone)
- [![Filters](https://polaris-react.shopify.com/_next/image?url=%2Fimages%2Fcomponents%2Fselection-and-input%2Ffilters.png&w=3840&q=75)\\
\\
**Filters** \\
A composite component that filters the items of a list or table.](https://polaris-react.shopify.com/components/selection-and-input/filters)
- [![Form](https://polaris-react.shopify.com/_next/image?url=%2Fimages%2Fcomponents%2Fselection-and-input%2Fform.png&w=3840&q=75)\\
\\
**Form** \\
A wrapper component that handles the submission of forms.](https://polaris-react.shopify.com/components/selection-and-input/form)
- [![Index filters](https://polaris-react.shopify.com/_next/image?url=%2Fimages%2Fcomponents%2Fselection-and-input%2Findex-filters.png&w=3840&q=75)\\
\\
**Index filters** \\
Use index filters to allow merchants to filter, search, and sort their index table data and create unique saved views from the results.](https://polaris-react.shopify.com/components/selection-and-input/index-filters)
- [![Inline error](https://polaris-react.shopify.com/_next/image?url=%2Fimages%2Fcomponents%2Fselection-and-input%2Finline-error.png&w=3840&q=75)\\
\\
**Inline error** \\
Inline errors are brief, in-context messages that tell merchants something went wrong with a single or group of inputs in a form. Use inline errors to help merchants understand why a form input may not be valid and how to fix it.](https://polaris-react.shopify.com/components/selection-and-input/inline-error)
- [![Radio button](https://polaris-react.shopify.com/_next/image?url=%2Fimages%2Fcomponents%2Fselection-and-input%2Fradio-button.png&w=3840&q=75)\\
\\
**Radio button** \\
Use radio buttons to present each item in a list of options where merchants must make a single selection.](https://polaris-react.shopify.com/components/selection-and-input/radio-button)
- [![Range slider](https://polaris-react.shopify.com/_next/image?url=%2Fimages%2Fcomponents%2Fselection-and-input%2Frange-slider.png&w=3840&q=75)\\
\\
**Range slider** \\
A range slider is an input field that merchants can use to select a numeric value within a given range (minimum and maximum values).](https://polaris-react.shopify.com/components/selection-and-input/range-slider)
- [![Select](https://polaris-react.shopify.com/_next/image?url=%2Fimages%2Fcomponents%2Fselection-and-input%2Fselect.png&w=3840&q=75)\\
\\
**Select** \\
Select lets merchants choose one option from an options menu. Consider select when you have 4 or more options, to avoid cluttering the interface.](https://polaris-react.shopify.com/components/selection-and-input/select)
- [![Tag](https://polaris-react.shopify.com/_next/image?url=%2Fimages%2Fcomponents%2Fselection-and-input%2Ftag.png&w=3840&q=75)\\
\\
**Tag** \\
Tags represent a set of interactive, merchant-supplied keywords that help label, organize, and categorize objects. Tags can be added or removed from an object by merchants.](https://polaris-react.shopify.com/components/selection-and-input/tag)
- [![Text field](https://polaris-react.shopify.com/_next/image?url=%2Fimages%2Fcomponents%2Fselection-and-input%2Ftext-field.png&w=3840&q=75)\\
\\
**Text field** \\
A text field is an input field that merchants can type into. It has a range of options and supports several text formats including numbers.](https://polaris-react.shopify.com/components/selection-and-input/text-field)

## Images and icons

- [![Avatar](https://polaris-react.shopify.com/_next/image?url=%2Fimages%2Fcomponents%2Fimages-and-icons%2Favatar.png&w=3840&q=75)\\
\\
**Avatar** \\
Used to show a thumbnail representation of an individual or business in the interface.](https://polaris-react.shopify.com/components/images-and-icons/avatar)
- [![Icon](https://polaris-react.shopify.com/_next/image?url=%2Fimages%2Fcomponents%2Fimages-and-icons%2Ficon.png&w=3840&q=75)\\
\\
**Icon** \\
Used to visually communicate core parts of the product and available actions, acting as wayfinding tools.](https://polaris-react.shopify.com/components/images-and-icons/icon)
- [![Keyboard key](https://polaris-react.shopify.com/_next/image?url=%2Fimages%2Fcomponents%2Fimages-and-icons%2Fkeyboard-key.png&w=3840&q=75)\\
\\
**Keyboard key** \\
Used to educate merchants about keyboard shortcuts.](https://polaris-react.shopify.com/components/images-and-icons/keyboard-key)
- [![Thumbnail](https://polaris-react.shopify.com/_next/image?url=%2Fimages%2Fcomponents%2Fimages-and-icons%2Fthumbnail.png&w=3840&q=75)\\
\\
**Thumbnail** \\
Used as a visual anchor and identifier for an object, along with text to provide context.](https://polaris-react.shopify.com/components/images-and-icons/thumbnail)
- [![Video thumbnail](https://polaris-react.shopify.com/_next/image?url=%2Fimages%2Fcomponents%2Fimages-and-icons%2Fvideo-thumbnail.png&w=3840&q=75)\\
\\
**Video thumbnail** \\
A clickable placeholder image that opens a video player within a modal or full screen when clicked.](https://polaris-react.shopify.com/components/images-and-icons/video-thumbnail)

## Feedback indicators

- [![Badge](https://polaris-react.shopify.com/_next/image?url=%2Fimages%2Fcomponents%2Ffeedback-indicators%2Fbadge.png&w=3840&q=75)\\
\\
**Badge** \\
Used to inform merchants of the tone of an object or an action taken.](https://polaris-react.shopify.com/components/feedback-indicators/badge)
- [![Banner](https://polaris-react.shopify.com/_next/image?url=%2Fimages%2Fcomponents%2Ffeedback-indicators%2Fbanner.png&w=3840&q=75)\\
\\
**Banner** \\
Informs merchants about important changes or persistent conditions in a prominent way.](https://polaris-react.shopify.com/components/feedback-indicators/banner)
- [![Exception list](https://polaris-react.shopify.com/_next/image?url=%2Fimages%2Fcomponents%2Ffeedback-indicators%2Fexception-list.png&w=3840&q=75)\\
\\
**Exception list** \\
Helps merchants notice important, standout information that adds extra context to a task.](https://polaris-react.shopify.com/components/feedback-indicators/exception-list)
- [![Progress bar](https://polaris-react.shopify.com/_next/image?url=%2Fimages%2Fcomponents%2Ffeedback-indicators%2Fprogress-bar.png&w=3840&q=75)\\
\\
**Progress bar** \\
Used to visually represent the completion of a task or operation.](https://polaris-react.shopify.com/components/feedback-indicators/progress-bar)
- [![Skeleton body text](https://polaris-react.shopify.com/_next/image?url=%2Fimages%2Fcomponents%2Ffeedback-indicators%2Fskeleton-body-text.png&w=3840&q=75)\\
\\
**Skeleton body text** \\
Provides a low fidelity representation of content before it appears, improving perceived load times.](https://polaris-react.shopify.com/components/feedback-indicators/skeleton-body-text)
- [![Skeleton display text](https://polaris-react.shopify.com/_next/image?url=%2Fimages%2Fcomponents%2Ffeedback-indicators%2Fskeleton-display-text.png&w=3840&q=75)\\
\\
**Skeleton display text** \\
Provides a low fidelity representation of content before it appears, improving perceived load times.](https://polaris-react.shopify.com/components/feedback-indicators/skeleton-display-text)
- [![Skeleton page](https://polaris-react.shopify.com/_next/image?url=%2Fimages%2Fcomponents%2Ffeedback-indicators%2Fskeleton-page.png&w=3840&q=75)\\
\\
**Skeleton page** \\
Used with other skeleton loading components to provide a low fidelity representation of the UI before content appears.](https://polaris-react.shopify.com/components/feedback-indicators/skeleton-page)
- [![Skeleton tabs](https://polaris-react.shopify.com/_next/image?url=%2Fimages%2Fcomponents%2Ffeedback-indicators%2Fskeleton-tabs.png&w=3840&q=75)\\
\\
**Skeleton tabs** \\
Provides a low fidelity representation of content before it appears, improving perceived load times.](https://polaris-react.shopify.com/components/feedback-indicators/skeleton-tabs)
- [![Skeleton thumbnail](https://polaris-react.shopify.com/_next/image?url=%2Fimages%2Fcomponents%2Ffeedback-indicators%2Fskeleton-thumbnail.png&w=3840&q=75)\\
\\
**Skeleton thumbnail** \\
Provides a low fidelity representation of an image before it appears, improving perceived load times.](https://polaris-react.shopify.com/components/feedback-indicators/skeleton-thumbnail)
- [![Spinner](https://polaris-react.shopify.com/_next/image?url=%2Fimages%2Fcomponents%2Ffeedback-indicators%2Fspinner.png&w=3840&q=75)\\
\\
**Spinner** \\
Used to notify merchants that their action is being processed. Used for content that can’t be represented with skeleton loading components.](https://polaris-react.shopify.com/components/feedback-indicators/spinner)

## Typography

- [![Text](https://polaris-react.shopify.com/_next/image?url=%2Fimages%2Fcomponents%2Ftypography%2Ftext.png&w=3840&q=75)\\
\\
**Text** \\
Typography helps establish hierarchy and communicate important content by creating clear visual patterns.](https://polaris-react.shopify.com/components/typography/text)

## Tables

- [![Data table](https://polaris-react.shopify.com/_next/image?url=%2Fimages%2Fcomponents%2Ftables%2Fdata-table.png&w=3840&q=75)\\
\\
**Data table** \\
Used to organize and display all information from a data set. Aimed to be as simple as possible for merchants.](https://polaris-react.shopify.com/components/tables/data-table)
- [![Index table](https://polaris-react.shopify.com/_next/image?url=%2Fimages%2Fcomponents%2Ftables%2Findex-table.png&w=3840&q=75)\\
\\
**Index table** \\
An index table displays a collection of objects of the same type, like orders or products. The main job of an index table is to help merchants get an at-a-glance of the objects to perform actions or navigate to a full-page representation of it.](https://polaris-react.shopify.com/components/tables/index-table)

## Lists

- [![Action list](https://polaris-react.shopify.com/_next/image?url=%2Fimages%2Fcomponents%2Flists%2Faction-list.png&w=3840&q=75)\\
\\
**Action list** \\
Action lists render a list of actions or selectable options. This component is usually placed inside a popover container to create a dropdown menu or to let merchants select from a list of options.](https://polaris-react.shopify.com/components/lists/action-list)
- [![Description list](https://polaris-react.shopify.com/_next/image?url=%2Fimages%2Fcomponents%2Flists%2Fdescription-list.png&w=3840&q=75)\\
\\
**Description list** \\
Used to present pairs of related information, like terms and definitions, or names and values, in a list format.](https://polaris-react.shopify.com/components/lists/description-list)
- [![List](https://polaris-react.shopify.com/_next/image?url=%2Fimages%2Fcomponents%2Flists%2Flist.png&w=3840&q=75)\\
\\
**List** \\
Lists display a set of related text-only content. Each list item begins with a bullet or a number.](https://polaris-react.shopify.com/components/lists/list)
- [![Listbox](https://polaris-react.shopify.com/_next/image?url=%2Fimages%2Fcomponents%2Flists%2Flistbox.png&w=3840&q=75)\\
\\
**Listbox** \\
A Listbox is a vertical list of interactive options, with room for icons, descriptions, and other elements.](https://polaris-react.shopify.com/components/lists/listbox)
- [![Option list](https://polaris-react.shopify.com/_next/image?url=%2Fimages%2Fcomponents%2Flists%2Foption-list.png&w=3840&q=75)\\
\\
**Option list** \\
The option list component lets you create a list of grouped items that merchants can pick from. This can include single selection or multiple selection of options. Option list usually appears in a popover, and sometimes in a modal or a sidebar. Option lists are styled differently than choice lists and should not be used within a form, but as a standalone menu.](https://polaris-react.shopify.com/components/lists/option-list)
- [![Resource item](https://polaris-react.shopify.com/_next/image?url=%2Fimages%2Fcomponents%2Flists%2Fresource-item.png&w=3840&q=75)\\
\\
**Resource item** \\
Resource items represent specific objects within a collection, such as products or orders. They provide contextual information on the resource type and link to the object’s detail page.](https://polaris-react.shopify.com/components/lists/resource-item)
- [![Resource list](https://polaris-react.shopify.com/_next/image?url=%2Fimages%2Fcomponents%2Flists%2Fresource-list.png&w=3840&q=75)\\
\\
**Resource list** \\
A resource list displays a collection of objects of the same type, like products or customers. The main job of a resource list is to help merchants find an object and navigate to a full-page representation of it.](https://polaris-react.shopify.com/components/lists/resource-list)

## Navigation

- [![Footer help](https://polaris-react.shopify.com/_next/image?url=%2Fimages%2Fcomponents%2Fnavigation%2Ffooter-help.png&w=3840&q=75)\\
\\
**Footer help** \\
Footer help is used to refer merchants to more information related to the product or feature they’re using.](https://polaris-react.shopify.com/components/navigation/footer-help)
- [![Link](https://polaris-react.shopify.com/_next/image?url=%2Fimages%2Fcomponents%2Fnavigation%2Flink.png&w=3840&q=75)\\
\\
**Link** \\
Links take users to another place, and usually appear within or directly following a sentence.](https://polaris-react.shopify.com/components/navigation/link)
- [![Pagination](https://polaris-react.shopify.com/_next/image?url=%2Fimages%2Fcomponents%2Fnavigation%2Fpagination.png&w=3840&q=75)\\
\\
**Pagination** \\
Use pagination to let merchants move through an ordered collection of items that has been split into pages. On the web, pagination uses buttons to move back and forth between pages. On iOS and Android, pagination uses infinite scrolling.](https://polaris-react.shopify.com/components/navigation/pagination)
- [![Tabs](https://polaris-react.shopify.com/_next/image?url=%2Fimages%2Fcomponents%2Fnavigation%2Ftabs.png&w=3840&q=75)\\
\\
**Tabs** \\
Used to alternate among related views within the same context.](https://polaris-react.shopify.com/components/navigation/tabs)

## Overlays

- [![Popover](https://polaris-react.shopify.com/_next/image?url=%2Fimages%2Fcomponents%2Foverlays%2Fpopover.png&w=3840&q=75)\\
\\
**Popover** \\
Small overlays that open on demand and close when the merchant interacts with any other part of Shopify. Used to surface secondary information or actions.](https://polaris-react.shopify.com/components/overlays/popover)
- [![Tooltip](https://polaris-react.shopify.com/_next/image?url=%2Fimages%2Fcomponents%2Foverlays%2Ftooltip.png&w=3840&q=75)\\
\\
**Tooltip** \\
Tooltips are floating labels that briefly explain the function of a user interface element. They can be triggered when merchants hover, focus, tap, or click.](https://polaris-react.shopify.com/components/overlays/tooltip)

## Utilities

- [![App provider](https://polaris-react.shopify.com/_next/image?url=%2Fimages%2Fcomponents%2Futilities%2Fapp-provider.png&w=3840&q=75)\\
\\
**App provider** \\
App provider is a required component that enables sharing global settings throughout the hierarchy of your application.](https://polaris-react.shopify.com/components/utilities/app-provider)
- [![Collapsible](https://polaris-react.shopify.com/_next/image?url=%2Fimages%2Fcomponents%2Futilities%2Fcollapsible.png&w=3840&q=75)\\
\\
**Collapsible** \\
Hides content and allows merchants to expand it. Used to hide optional settings, information, and actions.](https://polaris-react.shopify.com/components/utilities/collapsible)
- [![Scrollable](https://polaris-react.shopify.com/_next/image?url=%2Fimages%2Fcomponents%2Futilities%2Fscrollable.png&w=3840&q=75)\\
\\
**Scrollable** \\
Used in components with too much content for the available vertical space. Embeds long-form content in components like modals and popovers.](https://polaris-react.shopify.com/components/utilities/scrollable)

## Deprecated
